case_name: Phelps v. Dr. Joseph T. West

summary.brief: Phelps v. Dr. Joseph T. West. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Dr. West performed a catheterization on Mr. Phelps on August 22, 2014.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. West discovered that three of Mr. Phelps’ coronary arteries were blocked, including 95% of the right coronary artery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. West diagnosed Mr. Phelps with multi-level coronary artery disease and determined that he needed to undergo coronary artery bypass graft (CABG) surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On August 29, 2014, Mr. Phelps met with the surgeon, Dr. Paul Davis, who agreed with Dr. West that Mr. Phelps should undergo CABG surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On September 19, 2014, before the surgery could be performed, Mr. Phelps developed an acute myocardial infarction (MI) and went into cardiac arrest.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He died the following day, September 20, 2014.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: It is Dr. Fifer’s opinion that Dr. West complied with the applicable standards of care at all times hereto.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Fifer

evidence_index.text: Dr. Fifer believes that it was reasonable for Dr. West to discharge Mr. Phelps after his diagnostic catheterization based on the findings during the catheterization and Mr. Phelps’ clinical scenario.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Fifer

evidence_index.text: Dr. Fifer will also opine that it is not the standard of care to keep patients in the hospital when they have stable angina.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Fifer

evidence_index.text: The jury found Defendants not liable. The jury awarded no damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: NOW, THEREFORE, IT IS HEREBY ORDERED that the judgment of the Superior Court be, and the same is hereby, AFFIRMED.
evidence_index.speaker: Justice Valihura

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Mr. Phelps was referred by his regular cardiologist to Dr. West for further evaluation. On August 22, 2014, Dr. West performed a cardiac catheterization and discovered significant coronary artery blockages.

focused_exam.cardiovascular: Stable angina, no ongoing chest pain at rest

critical_findings: 95% blockage of the right coronary artery

requestables.name: Cardiac Catheterization
requestables.description: Diagnostic cardiac catheterization to assess coronary artery blockages

reveal.result_summary: Three coronary arteries blocked, including 95% of the right coronary artery
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates severe coronary artery disease requiring surgical intervention
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt consideration for immediate surgical intervention

requestables.name: Cardiac Surgery Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with cardiac surgeon to discuss surgical options

reveal.clinical_significance: Confirms need for coronary artery bypass graft surgery
reveal.decision_impact: Surgery scheduled but delayed due to patient condition and scheduling issues

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation and Workup
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents for cardiac evaluation and undergoes catheterization

timeline_phases.name: Follow-up and Complications
timeline_phases.description: Patient discharged and scheduled for surgery, develops complications

decision_points.clinical_context: Post-cardiac catheterization with significant coronary artery blockages
decision_points.prompt: Should the patient be discharged or remain hospitalized for immediate surgery?

options.description: Discharge the patient with instructions to follow up for surgery
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient had stable angina and no immediate symptoms indicating unstable angina

options.description: Admit the patient for immediate surgery
options.clinical_reasoning: Immediate surgery could prevent potential complications from severe blockages

actual_action_defendant.description: Patient was discharged with follow-up for surgery
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Patient had stable angina and was informed of risks and benefits

expected_action_court.description: Immediate surgery should have been considered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Plaintiffs alleged immediate surgery was necessary due to severity of blockages

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Plaintiffs argued that immediate surgery was necessary to prevent risk of MI
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been admitted for immediate surgery
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as debated in court

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered a myocardial infarction and died

key_findings: Dr. West complied with the standard of care
Discharge was appropriate given stable angina

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Immediate surgery might have prevented MI

factual_timeline.event: Cardiac catheterization performed by Dr. West
Consultation with Dr. Davis, surgery scheduled

tests_performed.result: Three coronary arteries blocked, including 95% of the right coronary artery

treatments.outcome: Patient scheduled for surgery but suffered MI before it could be performed

court_findings.finding: Dr. West complied with the standard of care
